fields.label: Choose cursor style for all players.
Check this box and click the green button to reset the game.

player_aid.text:
• 0vp if 0 or 1 adjacent color
 • Sum adjacent colors & scoring mill
 • +5 surrounded by 3 different color fields
 • +/-5 if gov and >= next guilder value

 • 20/15/10/5vp for 1/2/3/4th marked harvests
 • 10vp for most taken governors
 • -5 per governor on mills

governors_harvest_aid.detail:
Governors & Harvest Marking Information
Governors: Each time a black circle is revealed at the top of an island tile, place a governor token onto the mill that just revealed it. When a mill with a governor is scored, check the number on the next coin track spot to be covered. If the mill scores equal or more points to that number, you score an additional 5 points and take the governor into your area (placing on the bottom-right holder). If not, lose 5 points and move the governor to the next closest mill (if a tie, current player chooses).

At the end of the game, the player with the most taken governors gains 10vp. If there is a tie, all tied players gain 5vp. Any governors still on mills will lose the mill-owning players 5 points.

Harvest Marking: After scoring a mill, but before considering a governor for that scoring, you may mark that score by clicking on your harvest #1 or #2 and inputting that score. You can only mark twice per game. You CANNOT mark the same number as an opponent, so if there is a tie then decrease your number until there is not a tie and mark that value.

At the end of the game the highest marked harvest gains that player 20vp, second highest gains 15vp, then 10vp, and finally 5vp. The same player may score twice.

on_your_turn_aid.detail:
Gameplay Information
ON YOUR TURN
1. Move On Both Rondels
First, move your meeple zero or more spaces clockwise on the outer rondel, not exceeding the last space with a grey coin. Then move the inner rondel pawn that number of spaces PLUS one (do not skip empty spaces).

2. Place a Tile
Take the tile under the inner-rondel pawn and place it onto the board:

Mill Tile: Place the tile onto a green space that is adjacent to any flower or mill already on the board. After placing, put one of your windmill tokens (colored disc) on top of the mill. If you do not have any unused windmill tokens, leave the windmill without a token on the board. If the placed windmill is adjacent to any number of blue island (?) tiles, flip them face up. If the revealed tile has a black governor icon on the top, place a governor token onto the newly placed mill. If the revealed tile has a coin icon, take a coin from the middle of the rondels area.

Flower Tile: Place the tile onto a green space that is adjacent to one of your mill tiles (has your token on it). If this is not possible, place the flower tile onto a green space adjacent to any flower or mill tile already on the board.

3. Score Mills
At this point, every mill that has no green adjacent spaces scores (starting with the active player and moving clockwise). The player gains points equal to the scoring mill tile value and the value of each adjacent flower tile as long as there are at least TWO different adjacent flower colors (otherwise, score zero points). Score an additional 5 points if the mill is adjacent to all three flower colors. Potentially mark this harvest and score or lose points if there is a governor on the tile; see the Governors & Harvest Info box for more information.

4. Refill & End Turn
Fill the space on the inner rondel that is empty and does not have the pawn on it. Take the top tile of the matching stack (mill or flower) and place it on that space. If there is no matching tile, leave that space empty. The next clockwise player now takes their turn.

ENDGAME
Once a player moves the inner rondel pawn onto an empty space, the game ends immediately. Score Governors and Harvests according to the information in the box to the left.

phase1.detail:
: If this icon is showing in the top right, that means at least one other player is modifying a value (not necessarily the same as you) at the same time. This does not affect your value changes, but a notice won't be added to the history until all players have closed the modify value window. Click on the icon to forcibly close the modify value window for all players except for yourself.

Update button: Closes this window and updates your values according to the input change. This change will not show up in the history box until all players have closed the modify value window.

Change by: You may type a positive or negative value here, and this difference will automatically apply to the 'Set to' value next to it.

Set Total to: You may type any value here to indicate the total value, and this will automatically calculate the difference and show this as the 'Change by' value next to it.

+/- buttons: Click these buttons to add or subtract 1/5 value from the 'Set Total to' & 'Change by' values.

info.name: Flower Fields
info.attribution:
Layout, board, cards, design, coding, and library image by Jonathan Cox (email: Contact@GettingGames.com for inquiries) and released to the Public Domain under CC0.

All other icons are from assets with attribution in the About overlay.

The sound while changing turns is "Wrong Answer" by UNIVERSFIELD and is available under the Pixabay License at https://pixabay.com/sound-effects/wrong-answer-129254/.
info.description: Surround your mills with the best flowers, but beware others looking to use those flowers for themselves.
info.helpText:
Players take seats. Press Setup button to begin, which will automatically fill the flower market and randomly distribute the ? tiles on the board. A random player (indicated by a button next to the seat area) is chosen to start. To complete setup, in counter-player-order each player places one mill disk onto an empty "5" Windmill space on the board.

Player-color disks are used to indicate who controls a mill on the board.

To refill a flower or mill location on the market, click and drag the appropriate tile from their holder in the bottom-left.

Spend money by dragging it to the holder in the center of the flower market. Gain money by dragging it from the holder in the center of the flower market to your area.

Place a black governor token by dragging into the appropriate mill. Score governor tokens by dragging them to your area, placing on the bottom holder which will update the number of governors you have scored.

To reveal a "?" tile, left-click on it.

To modify points or harvest values, left-click on the value to modify and follow the instructions on the prompt.
